source_text: Classified Correspondence. Classified correspondence must be marked in accordance with SECNAV M-5510.30 (Department of the Navy Information Security Program). Classification markings (TOP SECRET, SECRET, CONFIDENTIAL) must appear on the first and last pages of the correspondence. The overall classification marking appears at the top and bottom of each page. Classified correspondence requires additional handling instructions and may require special distribution controls. Refer to SECNAV M-5510.30 and DoDM 5200.01 for specific classification marking requirements, placement, and handling procedures.
plain_language_rule: Classified correspondence must follow SECNAV M-5510.30 and DoDM 5200.01 marking requirements. Classification markings (TOP SECRET, SECRET, CONFIDENTIAL) must appear at the top and bottom of each page, with overall classification shown on first and last pages. Additional handling instructions and distribution controls may apply based on classification level.
renderer_impact: Classification marking placement at top/bottom of pages; may affect header/footer rendering; requires special handling for classified document workflows
notes: CRITICAL DEPENDENCY: This rule delegates authority to external security manuals (SECNAV M-5510.30, DoDM 5200.01). The SECNAV M-5216.5 correspondence manual does not specify detailed classification marking procedures - it references the Information Security Program manual for those requirements. ENFORCEABILITY is marked 'partial' because: (1) classification level determination depends on content analysis per security guidance, not document formatting; (2) specific marking placement details are in SECNAV M-5510.30, not this manual; (3) some classified correspondence handling depends on command-specific security procedures. The renderer should ensure classification markings appear at top and bottom of pages when present, but validation of proper classification level and complete marking compliance requires security program expertise beyond document formatting. This rule should remain candidate until coordination with security program officials confirms implementation approach. DO NOT activate without security program review.
